finding_results.notes: Replaced 'Like a high.' and 'Like stealing.' with 'You needed it that bad.' and 'And you just — kept taking it.' These arrive at the same recognition (addiction, predatory extraction) through colloquial, pressure-marked speech with a processing dash rather than clean two-word diagnoses. Replaced 'I'm not the route in.' with 'That's not — I can't take that for them.' which includes a false start and redirects responsibility concretely to Lena and Tomas without a quotable aphoristic closer. All replacements match Kai's style-bible register: contracted, physically anchored, shorter under pressure, finishing thoughts through addition rather than delivering miniature verdicts.

Replaced 'I ran her above the prescribed zone because the feed got cleaner there' with 'I ran her hot. Above the prescribed zone, every session. The feed sharpened when her load went up, and the desync made it worse. I told her the numbers were fine. They weren't.' This breaks the forbidden 'I [action] because [feed result]' scaffold by splitting the admission into separate declarative sentences where causality is implicit rather than bound by a 'because' clause. Replaced 'That wasn't enough for me. That's it.' with 'My own body wasn't enough for me.' which drops the declarative-capstone tag entirely and makes the admission more physical and body-anchored. Neither revised line echoes or near-echoes any style-bible example material.

summary: Two dialogue findings addressed. Kai's confrontation lines were roughened from composed two-word diagnoses and a quotable closer into pressure-marked speech with processing pauses, concrete referents, and false starts that match his style-bible register under strain. Margot's confession was rephrased to break the forbidden style-bible example scaffold: the 'because [intensification]' construction was split into separate declarative sentences, and the 'That's the answer' capstone was replaced with a more physical, body-anchored admission. All revisions preserve the scenes' dramatic work, character voice signatures, and chapter continuity without introducing new obligations or sanitizing the material.
